stem: A rectangular garden has an area of 200 square meters. If the length of the garden is twice the width, what is the width of the garden, in meters?
rationale: Let w be the width. Then the length is 2w, and the area is w · 2w = 2w² = 200. So w² = 100 and w = 10. Choice B is correct. Choice A (5) comes from dividing 200 by 40 by mistake. Choice C (20) is the length, not the width. Choice D (50) results from dividing the area by 4 incorrectly.
